method_atom: Treating the level sets (equal-value contour lines) of a rough two-dimensional scalar field as an ensemble of random curves and testing them for conformal invariance, then classifying those curves within a one-parameter family of stochastically-driven conformal growth processes indexed by a single diffusivity constant; matching the measured statistics to a known critical-interface universality class fixes that parameter, and a stochastic aggregation/growth rule is offered as a generative model reproducing the same large-scale contour statistics.
object_atom: The iso-height (equal-elevation) lines on the rough surface of a WO3 (tungsten trioxide) thin film, together with a ballistic-deposition surface-growth model proposed to generate contours with matching large-scale statistics.
brief_reason: The conformal-invariance testing and one-parameter stochastic-growth (SLE-style) classification of random contour lines is the transferable machinery, while the WO3 thin-film surface and its ballistic-deposition growth are the material-physics-specific object.
